text: Hello friends. This is a case of large dermoid cyst. This is approximately twenty year old girl unmarried and she has one mole around the umbilicus that is normal.

The surgeon performs a supraumbilical port placement approximately five centimeters above the umbilicus due to a large right-sided dermoid cyst. Irrigation and suction are used, followed by the hanging drop test for insufflation. The preset pressure is set to fifteen, with the actual pressure gradually increasing from single digits. A trocar is introduced using the baseball diamond concept, with ports positioned at snuff box locations. The dermo-axis is noted, and no uterine manipulator is used.

The surgeon uses an Ethicon endo catch bag, introducing it into the abdominal cavity to minimize spillage during the cystectomy. The endo bag is opened, and aspiration of the large dermoid cyst begins, collecting 5 to 10 ml of oily fluid for cytology. The aspiration needle is removed, and the incision is enlarged using a harmonic scalpel, with the endo bag positioned via the contralateral port. Two ipsilateral and one contralateral port are utilized in this procedure.
A 5mm suction device is introduced into the surgical field, rapidly aspirating the oily fluid content from the large dermoid cyst, causing the cyst to collapse.
The surgeon observes a small amount of spillage outside the endo bag and promptly uses suction to remove the spilled fluid from the surgical field.

The surgeon begins enucleating the dermoid cyst by stripping the inner cystic layer from the outer ovarian cortex. The cystic layer is visible, and the cortex is gradually separated using a Maryland dissector in one hand and a semi-traumatic grasper in the other. The surgeon alternates between holding and stripping with the instruments to achieve a clean separation.

The surgeon completes the stripping of the cyst from the ovary, with minor oozing observed. The cyst is placed into the endo bag on the right side, while the remaining ovary is visible on the left. The mouth of the endo bag is closed using a purse string and metal ring, securing the cyst inside. The endo bag is then pulled out through the port with gentle finger dilation.
So this is extraction in the endoval. Thank you very much for watching this video. This was just a simple case of Thermo-Acid System.
